title: Successful login redirects to dashboard
description: Verifies a user can log in with valid credentials and is redirected to the dashboard.

title: Invalid password shows error on login page
description: Verifies an invalid login attempt displays an error and does not navigate away from the login page.

title: Login validation: required fields prevent submission
description: Verifies the login form shows a visible validation message when required fields are missing.

title: Navigate from login to registration page
description: Verifies users can reach the registration page from the login screen.

title: Registration success shows confirmation/onboarding state
description: Verifies a user can submit the registration form and see a success confirmation/onboarding indicator.

steps.description: Type "Test Admin" into the full name field

title: Navigate from login to forgot password page
description: Verifies users can reach the forgot password page from the login screen.

title: Forgot password: request reset without email/phone shows validation
description: Verifies the forgot password form requires an email/phone before submission and shows a visible error if missing.

title: Add a new client and verify it appears in the client list
description: Verifies a user can create a new client and immediately see the new entry in the clients list.

steps.description: Click "Clients" in the main navigation menu
Fill "Test Client" into the client name field
Select "Hotspot" from the client type dropdown

title: Client creation form shows validation when required fields are missing
description: Verifies the add client form blocks saving and shows a visible validation message when required data is omitted.

steps.description: Click "Clients" in the main navigation menu
Fill "Validation Client" into the client name field

title: Ban an existing client from the list and verify status changes to Banned
description: Verifies an admin can ban a client via the Actions menu and the client status updates in the list.

steps.description: Click "Clients" in the main navigation menu
Click the "Actions" button/menu for the first client row in the list

title: Cancel banning a client and verify status does not change to Banned
description: Verifies that choosing cancel/close on the ban confirmation does not apply the status change.

steps.description: Click "Clients" in the main navigation menu
Click the "Actions" button/menu for the first client row in the list

title: Filter clients by Expired and extend an expired subscription
description: Verifies an admin can filter to expired subscribers, extend a subscription, and see the updated expiry information.

steps.description: Click "Clients" in the main navigation menu
Select "Expired" from the status filter dropdown
Click the first client row in the filtered results
Fill "30" into the extension days field

title: Extend subscription shows validation when extension details are missing
description: Verifies the extend subscription workflow prevents saving and shows a visible validation message when required extension inputs are empty.

steps.description: Click "Clients" in the main navigation menu
Select "Expired" from the status filter dropdown
Click the first client row in the filtered results

title: Change client status to Disabled from Actions menu
description: Verifies an admin can change a client status to Disabled and see the updated status in the list.

steps.description: Click "Clients" in the main navigation menu
Click the "Actions" button/menu for the first client row in the list

title: Change client status to Suspended from Actions menu
description: Verifies an admin can suspend a client and see status reflected as Suspended in the client list.

steps.description: Click "Clients" in the main navigation menu
Click the "Actions" button/menu for the first client row in the list

title: Dashboard shows revenue analytics and subscriber growth summary on initial load
description: Verifies that the dashboard loads and displays key revenue and subscriber growth widgets.

title: Dashboard displays revenue charts widget
description: Verifies that the revenue charts area is visible after successful login and dashboard load.

title: Dashboard shows router status summary with Online and Offline counts
description: Verifies that router status summary section is present and includes Online/Offline indicators.

title: Dashboard system monitor section is visible (CPU, Memory, Disk, Traffic)
description: Verifies the system monitor widget is present and displays the key resource labels.

title: Dashboard network traffic values appear after loading
description: Verifies that network traffic values in the system monitor load and are visible to the user.

steps.description: Wait for element "Traffic" to be visible

title: Filter dashboard metrics by router using 'Filter by Router' dropdown
description: Verifies that selecting a specific router in the filter updates the dashboard view to a filtered state.

steps.description: Select the first non-default router option from the dropdown

title: Filter by Router can be changed to another router selection
description: Verifies that changing the router filter selection updates the dashboard again (second refresh).

steps.description: Select the first non-default router option from the dropdown
Select a different router option from the dropdown

title: Dashboard shows error/fallback UI and Retry button when data load fails
description: Verifies that the dashboard displays an error state with a Retry control when the dashboard data cannot be loaded.

title: Retry reloads dashboard after an error state
description: Verifies that clicking Retry re-attempts loading and returns to a normal dashboard view when successful.

steps.description: Wait for element "Revenue chart" to be visible

title: Create a new router with a selected VPN mode and view setup instructions
description: Verifies a router can be created from the Routers page and the setup instructions/download links are shown after creation.

steps.description: Click on "Routers" in the main navigation menu
Click the "+ Add New Router" button

title: Complete router creation form and confirm setup wizard content is displayed
description: Verifies the add-router dialog accepts valid inputs, creates the router, and shows setup wizard instructions and configuration links.

steps.description: Click on "Routers" in the main navigation menu
Click the "+ Add New Router" button

title: Select VPN configuration mode and create router
description: Verifies VPN mode selection works and the router can be created, resulting in visible setup instructions.

steps.description: Click on "Routers" in the main navigation menu
Click the "+ Add New Router" button
Select "WireGuard" from the "VPN Configuration Mode" dropdown

title: Router name validation rejects invalid characters
description: Verifies entering a router name with invalid characters prevents creation and shows a visible validation error.

steps.description: Click on "Routers" in the main navigation menu
Click the "+ Add New Router" button

title: Router name validation error is displayed and router is not created
description: Verifies a clear error message is shown for invalid router name and the add-router flow does not proceed to setup instructions.

steps.description: Click on "Routers" in the main navigation menu
Click the "+ Add New Router" button

title: Create a new Hotspot package and verify it appears in the package list
description: Verifies a user can create a Hotspot service package with price, duration, router, and bandwidth settings and then see it listed.

steps.description: Click "Packages" in the main navigation menu
Select "Hotspot" from the Package Type options
Type "Hotspot Basic Plan" into the Name field
Select an option from the Router dropdown
Select an option from the Duration dropdown
Type "5" into the Upload Speed field
Type "10" into the Download Speed field
Click the "+ Add Package" button on the form
Verify text "Hotspot Basic Plan" is visible

title: Create a new PPPoE package and verify it appears in the package list
description: Verifies a user can create a PPPoE service package and see it listed after submission.

steps.description: Click "Packages" in the main navigation menu
Select "PPPoE" from the Package Type options
Type "PPPoE Standard" into the Name field
Select an option from the Router dropdown
Select an option from the Duration dropdown
Type "10" into the Upload Speed field
Type "20" into the Download Speed field
Click the "+ Add Package" button on the form

title: Create a new Static IP package and verify it appears in the package list
description: Verifies a user can create a Static IP service package and confirm it is shown in the list.

steps.description: Click "Packages" in the main navigation menu
Select "Static" from the Package Type options
Type "Static IP Bronze" into the Name field
Select an option from the Router dropdown
Select an option from the Duration dropdown
Type "15" into the Upload Speed field
Type "30" into the Download Speed field
Click the "+ Add Package" button on the form
Verify text "Static IP Bronze" is visible

title: Show validation error when creating a package with missing price
description: Verifies the form blocks submission and shows a required-field error when Price is left empty.

steps.description: Click "Packages" in the main navigation menu
Type "No Price Plan" into the Name field
Click the "+ Add Package" button on the form
Verify text "Price is required" is visible

title: Edit an existing package upload speed and verify the update is reflected in the list
description: Verifies a user can edit a package, save changes, and see updated upload speed in the package list.

steps.description: Click "Packages" in the main navigation menu
Click the "Edit" action for the first package in the package list
Type "12" into the Upload Speed field

title: Filter packages by Inactive status
description: Verifies selecting the Inactive status filter limits results to inactive packages.

steps.description: Click "Packages" in the main navigation menu
Select "Inactive" from the status filter options

title: Enable M-Pesa gateway with valid credentials and save
description: Verifies that a user can enable the M-Pesa gateway, enter required credentials, save, and see a success confirmation.

steps.description: Click on "Transactions" in the main navigation menu
Click the M-Pesa gateway toggle to set it to "Enable"

title: M-Pesa enablement shows validation error when Consumer Key is missing
description: Verifies that saving M-Pesa configuration with an empty Consumer Key shows a required-field validation error.

steps.description: Click on "Transactions" in the main navigation menu
Click the M-Pesa gateway toggle to set it to "Enable"
Verify text "Consumer Key is required" is visible

title: Register C2B URL from Payment Setup shows success message
description: Verifies that the user can initiate C2B URL registration and see a successful registration message.

steps.description: Click on "Transactions" in the main navigation menu
Wait for a registration result message to appear
Verify text "C2B URL registered" is visible

title: Configure HarakaPay API key, save, and run Test Payment to see response
description: Verifies that HarakaPay can be configured, saved, and a test payment can be initiated with visible response and verification status.

steps.description: Click on "Transactions" in the main navigation menu
Type "harakapay-test-api-key" into the "HarakaPay API Key" field
Wait for the test payment result to appear

title: Create a new voucher and verify it appears in the vouchers table
description: Verifies an authenticated user can add a voucher with plan name, code, and router and then see it listed.

steps.description: Click "Transactions" in the main navigation menu
Click the "Vouchers" section/tab on the Transactions page
Type "Test Plan A" into the "Plan Name" field
Select the first available option from the "Router" dropdown
Verify text "AUTO-VOUCHER-001" is visible in the Vouchers table

title: Filter vouchers by a specific router
description: Verifies filtering by a router updates the vouchers list to that router's vouchers.

steps.description: Click "Transactions" in the main navigation menu
Click the "Vouchers" section/tab on the Transactions page
Select a specific router (not "(all routers)") from the Router filter dropdown

title: Clear router filter by selecting (all routers)
description: Verifies selecting (all routers) shows vouchers across routers after previously filtering.

steps.description: Click "Transactions" in the main navigation menu
Click the "Vouchers" section/tab on the Transactions page
Select a specific router (not "(all routers)") from the Router filter dropdown
Select "(all routers)" from the Router filter dropdown

title: Validate required fields when adding a voucher (missing plan name and code)
description: Verifies the add voucher form blocks saving and shows validation messages when required fields are empty.

steps.description: Click "Transactions" in the main navigation menu
Click the "Vouchers" section/tab on the Transactions page
Verify text "Plan Name" is visible near a validation message or error state
Verify text "Voucher Code" is visible near a validation message or error state

title: Cancel out of Add voucher without saving
description: Verifies a user can exit the add voucher dialog/form without creating a voucher.

steps.description: Click "Transactions" in the main navigation menu
Click the "Vouchers" section/tab on the Transactions page

title: View transactions table and open a transaction details modal
description: Verifies the transactions log page loads a records table and that clicking a row opens a visible detail modal.

steps.description: Click "Transactions" in the main navigation menu
Verify a transactions table is visible
Click on the first transaction row in the table
Verify element "Transaction details" modal is visible

title: Filter transactions by Failed status and verify only failed results are shown
description: Verifies filtering by status narrows the table results to Failed transactions.

steps.description: Click "Transactions" in the main navigation menu
Select "Failed" from the Status filter dropdown
Wait for the transactions table to update
Verify text "Failed" is visible in the transactions table

title: Manually add a new transaction and verify it appears in the table
description: Verifies a user can add a transaction via the add transaction flow and see it listed in the transactions table.

steps.description: Click "Transactions" in the main navigation menu
Verify element "Add Transaction" form is visible

title: Search by Transaction ID with no matches
description: Verifies searching with an invalid/non-existent Transaction ID shows a zero-results state message.

steps.description: Click "Transactions" in the main navigation menu

title: Close transaction details modal after opening it
description: Verifies the transaction detail modal can be dismissed after being opened from a row.

steps.description: Click "Transactions" in the main navigation menu
Click on the first transaction row in the table
Verify element "Transaction details" modal is visible
Click the "Close" button in the modal

title: Clear status filter to return to full transaction list
description: Verifies the user can remove the Failed filter and see the table return to an unfiltered or broader result set.

steps.description: Click "Transactions" in the main navigation menu
Select "Failed" from the Status filter dropdown
Select "All" from the Status filter dropdown
Verify a transactions table is visible
